rationale: The LLM capability ceiling (simple resource exhaustion DoS via scripted HTTP requests) is substantially below the sophistication required for execution techniques relevant to this attack step — namely PowerShell obfuscation, AMSI bypass, process injection, and LOLBAS chaining to evade enterprise EDR. An OC3 actor already possesses core execution skills, so the marginal uplift from a low-ceiling LLM is limited primarily to syntax assistance, LOLBAS one-liner generation, and script iteration acceleration. This justifies a modest ~7 percentage point uplift from the 50% baseline, yielding a median estimate of approximately 57%.
